title: Playwright Rendering, Isolation, Readiness, and Dynamic-DOM Evidence · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds isolated evidence-driven rendering adapter from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of isolated evidence-driven rendering adapter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause.
Create bounded incognito-like BrowserContexts with explicit storage, permission, download, service-worker, locale, viewport, and close policy.
Wait for application-specific stable evidence under time and request budgets instead of treating network idle as universal readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Render Necessity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6009 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove necessity, context isolation, readiness, browser network policy, provenance, and closure; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Render Necessity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Render Necessity, keep Render Necessity connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Render Necessity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: JavaScript presence means every page requires a browser.

options.text: JavaScript presence means every page requires a browser. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.
State the Render Necessity preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
